parts.OCHRANA DÁLNIC, SILNIC A MÍSTNÍCH KOMUNI: PROTECTION OF MOTORWAYS, ROADS AND LOCAL ROADS AND THEIR INTERFACE WITH THE SURROUNDINGS
parts.SILNIČNÍ POZEMEK, SOUČÁSTI A PŘÍSLUŠENST: ROAD LAND, COMPONENTS AND APPURTENANCES OF MOTORWAYS, ROADS AND LOCAL ROADS
parts.VÝSTAVBA DÁLNICE, SILNICE A MÍSTNÍ KOMUN: CONSTRUCTION OF MOTORWAYS, ROADS AND LOCAL ROADS

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text: This Act regulates the categorization of roads, their construction, conditions of use and their protection, the rights and obligations of road owners and users, and the exercise of state administration in road matters by the competent road administrative authorities.

p2.heading: Roads and their classification
p2.text:
(1) A „road“ is a transport route intended for use by road and other vehicles^1) and pedestrians, including fixed facilities necessary to ensure such use and its safety.

(2) Roads are divided into the following categories:

- a) motorways,

- b) roads,

- c) local roads,

- d) special-purpose roads.

Categorization of roads into individual categories and classes and their changes

p3.text:
(1) The competent road administrative authority shall decide on the categorization of a road as a motorway, road or local road based on its designation, transport significance and structural and technical equipment.

(2) If the transport significance or designation of a road changes, the competent road administrative authority shall decide on a change of category.

(3) In cases where a change in the category of a road requires a change in ownership relations to the road, the competent road administrative authority may issue a decision on the change of category only on the basis of an agreement on a future agreement on the transfer of ownership rights to the affected road concluded between the existing owner and the future owner. Until the transfer of ownership rights to the affected road, all rights and obligations to this road shall be exercised by its current owner.

p4.heading: Motorways
p4.text:
(1) A „motorway“ is a road intended for fast long-distance and international transport by motor vehicles, which is constructed without at-grade crossings, with separate connection points for entry and exit, and which has directionally separated carriageways.

(2) A motorway is accessible only to motor vehicles whose maximum permitted speed is not lower than stipulated by a special regulation.^2)

p5.heading: Roads
p5.text:
(1) A „road“ is a publicly accessible road intended for use by road and other vehicles and pedestrians. Roads form the road network.

(2) Roads are divided into the following classes according to their designation and transport significance:

- a) Class I roads, which are intended primarily for long-distance and international transport,

- b) Class II roads, which are intended for transport between districts,

- c) Class III roads, which are intended for mutual connection of municipalities or their connection to other roads.

(3) A Class I road constructed as an expressway is intended for fast transport and is accessible only to motor vehicles whose maximum permitted speed is not lower than stipulated by a special regulation.^2) An expressway has similar structural and technical equipment to a motorway.

p6.heading: Local roads
p6.text:
(1) A „local road“ is a publicly accessible road that primarily serves local traffic within the territory of a municipality.

(2) A local road may be constructed as a fast local road, which is intended for fast traffic and accessible only to motor vehicles whose maximum permitted speed is not lower than stipulated by a special regulation.^2) A fast local road has similar structural and technical equipment to a motorway.

(3) Local roads are divided into the following classes according to their transport significance, designation and structural and technical equipment:

- a) Class I local roads, which are primarily fast local roads,

- b) Class II local roads, which are transport-significant collector roads with restricted direct connection of adjacent properties,

- c) Class III local roads, which are service roads,

- d) Class IV local roads, which are roads inaccessible to motor vehicle traffic or on which mixed traffic is permitted.

(4) The implementing regulation shall further define the characteristics for the division of local roads into individual classes.

p7.heading: Special-purpose roads
p7.text:
(1) A „special-purpose road“ is a road that serves to connect individual properties for the needs of the owners of these properties or to connect these properties with other roads or for the management of agricultural and forest land. The competent road administrative authority may, at the proposal of the owner of the special-purpose road and after consultation with the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic, regulate or restrict public access to the special-purpose road, if this is absolutely necessary to protect the legitimate interests of this owner.

(2) A special-purpose road also includes a road in an enclosed area or facility that serves the needs of the owner or operator of the enclosed area or facility. This special-purpose road is not publicly accessible, but to the extent and in the manner determined by the owner or operator of the enclosed area or facility. In case of doubt as to whether, from the perspective of a road, it is an enclosed area or facility, the competent road administrative authority shall decide.

p8.heading: Through section of a motorway and road
p8.text:
(1) Motorways and roads may run through built-up or developable areas (hereinafter referred to as a “motorway through-section” or a “road through-section”) if they primarily carry through traffic through such areas.

(2) If the boundary of a built-up or developable area is not included in the approved land-use planning documentation, the boundary of continuous built-up area for the purpose of defining the length of a motorway through-section or a road through-section shall be determined by the competent building authority, based on the construction and technical conditions of the area, upon the proposal of the competent road administration authority and after prior consultation with the municipality concerned.

(3) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the construction and technical conditions for determining the boundaries of a through-section pursuant to the preceding paragraph.

p9.heading: Ownership of land communications
p9.text:
(1) The owner of motorways and roads is the state. The owner of local roads is the municipality in whose territory the local roads are located. The owner of special-purpose roads is a legal or natural person.

(2) The ownership right of the state to motorways and roads shall be exercised by the Ministry of Transport and Communications.

(3) The Ministry of Transport and Communications or the municipality may entrust the exercise of ownership rights to a legal or natural person (hereinafter referred to as the “land communication administrator”).

(4) The implementing regulation shall define the details concerning the care of motorways, roads, and local roads by their owner, and their registration.

p10.heading: Connecting land communications
p10.text:
(1) Land communications may be connected to each other by establishing intersections or adjacent properties may be connected to them by establishing access roads or ramps. Direct connection of an adjacent property to a land communication is not a special-purpose road.

(2) Branches of grade-separated intersections and roundabouts are assigned to the land communication of a higher category or class; branches of at-grade intersections are assigned to the land communication of a lower category or class.

(3) Only a structure that exclusively serves the users of these land communications (e.g., a petrol station, a roadside restaurant, a motel, a car repair shop) may be directly connected to a motorway and an expressway from a rest area.

(4) Before issuing a permit,

- a) for the connection of a motorway, road, or local road to another land communication, for the modification of such connection, or for its cancellation, the competent road administration authority shall request prior consent from the owner of the land communication of a higher category or class,

- b) for the connection of an adjacent property to a motorway, road, or local road, for the modification of such connection, or for its cancellation, the competent road administration authority shall request prior consent from the owner of the affected land communication,

and if it concerns a motorway or an expressway, also prior consent from the Ministry of the Interior; in other cases, prior consent from the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic.

(5) The implementing regulation shall regulate the technical conditions for connecting land communications to each other and the conditions for connecting an adjacent property to a motorway, road, and local road.

p11.heading: Road land
p11.text:
(1) Road land consists of the body of a motorway, road, and local road, and auxiliary road land.

(2) The body of a motorway or the body of a road and local road outside built-up or developable areas is delimited by the lower edge and the outer edges of the land communication structure, which are the outer edges of rounded cut slopes or rounded embankment toes, the outer edges of road or catch ditches or gutters, or the outer edges of the toes of retaining walls, terraces, the crown of cladding or revetment walls, or cuts above these walls.

(3) The body of a road through-section is delimited by the width of the carriageway with verges between raised kerbs of pavements, green belts, or similar areas. In squares and similar open spaces, the width of the through-section is the width of the strip distinguished from the surrounding surface by the type or material of the carriageway or by flat gutters, and if there are none, the width of the strip corresponding to the width of the carriageway with verges of the adjacent road sections. In other cases, the width of a motorway through-section or a road through-section is delimited similarly to paragraph 2.

(4) The width of a motorway through-section or a road through-section at a customs post at a border crossing corresponds to the width of the carriageway with verges of the adjacent motorway or road sections.

(5) Auxiliary road land is a strip of land adjacent on both sides to the body of a motorway, road, or local road outside continuously built-up areas of municipalities, which serves the purposes of protection and maintenance of the motorway, road, or local road, provided that these lands are owned by the owner of the motorway, road, or local road.

Components and appurtenances

p12.text:
(1) The components of a motorway, road, and local road include:

- a) all structural layers of carriageways and verges, rest areas, auxiliary and additional lanes, including bus stop lanes for scheduled passenger transport,

- b) bridge structures (overpasses) carrying the road, including sidewalks, inspection facilities, protective shields and nets thereon, mechanical equipment of drawbridges, icebreakers, culverts, footbridges or cycle bridges,

- c) tunnels, galleries, retaining, revetment, cladding and parapet walls, terraces, embankments and slopes, median strips, ditches and other surface drainage facilities, road auxiliary land,

- d) railings, reflectors, crash barriers, resilient barriers, guide posts, road studs, mileposts, boundary markers, horizontal road markings, traffic islands, reflective and guide strips, and speed bumps,

- e) escape zones, noise barriers and noise mounds, if located on road land.

(2) If the carriageway structure is laid directly on the structure of another building (water structure, metro, hall, garages), only this carriageway is considered a component.

(3) Sewerage, including arrangements for water drainage, oil separators and sedimentation tanks, is a component of a motorway, road or local road only if it serves exclusively to drain surface water from that road. In other cases, only the storm drain with a manhole and connection to the sewerage system is a component.

(4) If they are not separate local roads, adjacent sidewalks, sidewalks under arcades, public car parks and turning areas, underpasses and facilities for ensuring and securing pedestrian crossings are also components of local roads.

(5) A rest area is a structurally and operationally defined area of a motorway, road or local road intended for the gratuitous parking of a road motor vehicle for the time necessary to ensure the safety and fluidity of road traffic and for the rest of users, or for their refreshment and refuelling.

(6) A public car park is a structurally and operationally defined area of a local or service road or a separate local or service road intended for the parking of a road motor vehicle.

(7) A lane or strip for cyclists is a component of the land communication on whose body it is located. A separate cycle path is, according to its nature and location, either a local road of IV. class or a service road.

p13.text:
The appurtenances of a motorway, road and local road include:

- a) vertical traffic signs and traffic devices,^2)

- b) ice detectors, call boxes and other devices for operational information,

- c) public lighting, traffic light signalling devices serving to control traffic,

- d) road vegetation, snow fences, storage facilities and dumps for maintenance materials,

- e) facilities and areas directly serving the maintenance of a motorway, road or local road (road maintenance depots) and their connection to the relevant land communication,

- f) devices preventing the entry of wild animals (e.g., fences, overpasses, tunnels),

- g) devices for paying the price for using a defined section of a local road.

p14.text:
(1) The provisions of § 12 and 13 apply to the components and appurtenances of a through section of a motorway and a through section of a road, with the following deviations:

- a) railings and similar devices are components only on bridge structures and on walls,

- b) railings, chains and other devices for ensuring and securing pedestrian crossings, public lighting, traffic light signalling devices serving to control traffic, and road vegetation are neither components nor appurtenances.

(2) The components or appurtenances of a motorway, road and local road do not include:

- a) exits or entrances to adjacent properties, dams of water reservoirs and ponds, banks of watercourses along which the road runs below the bank line, embankment walls built for watercourse regulation, water management structures under bridges, land reclamation facilities (culverts, underpasses),

- b) boarding islands, stop signs and waiting rooms for scheduled passenger transport and public transport, trolley lines and their poles, operational and technical police facilities,

- c) level crossings without barriers up to a distance of 2.5 m from the axis of the outermost track and level crossings with barriers within the distance between the barriers, devices for securing railway crossings, tram and railway track superstructure at road level up to a distance of 0.5 m from the outer edge of the rail, separate railway bodies,

- d) bus stations, motels, roadside restaurants, petrol stations and customs offices at border crossings.

(3) Furthermore, utility networks, energy, telecommunication, heating and other lines including their poles, if they do not serve exclusively the owner of the affected road, advertising boards and billboards of all kinds, stalls and other mobile or portable sales facilities are neither components nor appurtenances of motorways, roads and local roads.

p15.heading: Road vegetation
p15.text:
(1) Road vegetation on road auxiliary land and on other suitable land forming part of a motorway, road or local road must not endanger the safety of using the land communication or unduly impede the use of such land for the purposes of maintaining these communications or unduly impede the management of adjacent land.

(2) Upon the proposal of the relevant authority of the Police of the Czech Republic or after consultation with it, the owner of a motorway, road and local road is entitled to fell trees on road land in accordance with special regulations.^3)

p16.heading: Building permit procedure
p16.text:
(1) For the construction of a motorway, road or local road, the competent road administrative authority is the „special building authority“^4). The special building authority shall invite the Ministry of the Interior to the building proceedings if it concerns the construction of a motorway or expressway; in other cases, it shall invite the competent body of the Police of the Czech Republic.

(2) Unless otherwise provided for in this Act, special regulations on spatial planning and building code^5) shall apply to the spatial and building proceedings for the construction of a motorway, road or local road, and regulations on environmental protection^6) shall not be affected.

(3) The implementing regulation shall define for which building modifications of a motorway, road or local road carried out on a road plot a notification to the special building authority shall suffice instead of a building permit, and for which maintenance works such notification is not required. The implementing regulation shall further stipulate general technical requirements for the construction of a motorway, road and local road.

p17.heading: Easements
p17.text:
(1) If all conditions for the expropriation of an undeveloped plot of land necessary for the construction of a motorway, road or local road are met, and expropriation proceedings cannot be initiated or concluded by an expropriation decision because the affected owner is unknown or undetermined, or because they are demonstrably unreachable or inactive, the competent special building authority may, at the proposal of the builder of the motorway, road or local road, issue a decision on the establishment of an easement enabling the use of this plot of land, or part thereof, for the purposes of the construction.

(2) The decision according to the preceding paragraph shall also include the determination of the amount of compensation according to a special regulation.^7) The right corresponding to this easement is another right to the land which, according to special regulations,^8) entitles the builder to construct the required structure on the affected land.

(3) If a motorway, road or local road has been constructed on another person's land and the owner of this structure has demonstrably failed to achieve property settlement with the owner of the land, the competent special building authority is entitled, at the proposal of the owner of the structure, to establish an easement necessary for the exercise of the right of ownership to the structure, for a one-off compensation in the amount which, according to a special regulation,^7) is due for expropriation.

p18.heading: Abolition of a motorway, road or local road
p18.text: If the transport significance of a motorway, road or local road has ceased and no decision has been made on changing the category of the road, the competent road administrative authority shall decide on its abolition. The special building authority shall immediately decide on a change in the use of the structure or its removal.

p19.heading: General use
p19.text:
(1) Within the limits of special regulations governing road traffic^2) and under the conditions stipulated by this Act, everyone may use land communications free of charge in the usual manner and for the purposes for which they are intended (hereinafter referred to as “general use”), unless this Act or a special regulation^9) provides otherwise for specific cases. The user must adapt to the structural condition and traffic-technical condition of the affected land communication.

(2) It is prohibited to pollute or damage motorways, roads, and local communications, their components and accessories. Furthermore, on motorways, roads, and local communications, it is prohibited to

- a) unlawfully remove, cover, relocate, install or alter traffic signs and traffic equipment, or place anything on these items,

- b) use tracked and other vehicles whose wheels are not equipped with pneumatic tyres or rubber rims, or use other machines and equipment that may cause damage to the communication; exceptionally, the use of a road or local communication by tracked vehicles of the Army of the Czech Republic or historical vehicles may be permitted as special use (§ 25),

- c) use snow chains in sections where the road surface is not sufficiently covered with snow or ice,

- d) use studs in tyres, unless a special regulation^10) provides otherwise,

- e) discharge water, sewage, and other liquid waste,

- f) spread temporary stockpiles of maintenance materials, turn agricultural or forestry machinery and teams of animals when performing field or forestry work,

- g) park a road vehicle that is permanently technically unfit for operation^10) and is not equipped with a state registration plate^11) or that is obviously permanently technically unfit for operation (hereinafter referred to as a “wreck”).

(3) The owner of a wreck is obliged, upon the request of the owner of the motorway, road, or local communication, to remove the wreck within two months of the delivery of the request for removal. If they fail to do so, the owner of that land communication shall remove and dispose of the wreck at the expense of the owner of the wreck.

(4) If the owner of the motorway, road, or local communication cannot ascertain the owner of the wreck, they shall publish a request for the removal of the wreck in a manner customary in the locality and, after the fruitless expiry of a two-month period from the date of publication of the request, shall remove and dispose of the wreck at their own expense. If the owner of the motorway, road, or local communication subsequently ascertains the owner of the wreck, they may assert a claim against them for reimbursement of the costs incurred in the removal and disposal of the wreck.

(5) The road administrative authority is authorised to decide on the designation of a local communication or a through section of a road or their parts, on which the parking of road vehicles is temporarily prohibited due to public interest, with the relevant traffic sign according to a special regulation.^2) The decision on designation must be implemented at least one week before the day of the temporary parking prohibition on the relevant land communication, and in this case, the general regulations on administrative procedure do not apply to the proceedings of the road administrative authority.

(6) In the event of non-compliance with the decision of the road administrative authority according to the previous paragraph, the owner of the local communication or the through section of the road is authorised to remove the road vehicle at the expense of its owner, unless the owner of the road vehicle proves serious reasons that prevented them from removing the road vehicle in time themselves; in this case, the costs associated with the removal of the road vehicle shall be borne by the person at whose request the decision was issued by the road administrative authority.

Charging for general use

p23.text:
(1) Local communications or their sections defined by a generally binding municipal decree may be used for a price agreed upon in accordance with price regulations^12)

- a) for parking a motor vehicle for a limited period, but not exceeding 24 hours, or

- b) for parking a truck or a road train for the time necessary to ensure customs clearance,

provided that, according to the opinion of the relevant authority of the Police of the Czech Republic, road traffic safety and fluidity will not be restricted, nor will public interest be endangered.

(2) Municipalities may, by a generally binding decree, define local communications in city centres and city districts where the parking of motor vehicles is permitted only for persons with residence or registered office in the defined area, provided they meet the conditions stipulated by the generally binding municipal decree, and for a price agreed upon according to price regulations.^12) Payment of the price is evidenced by a card, which must be placed in a visible location behind the front windshield of the motor vehicle for the duration of its parking. The defined sections of local communications must be marked with the relevant traffic sign according to a special regulation.^2)

p24.heading: Restriction of general use by closures and detours
p24.text:
(1) Traffic on motorways, roads, local roads and publicly accessible účelové komunikace (access roads) may be partially or completely closed, or a diversion may be ordered. No one is entitled to compensation for any losses incurred as a result of the closure or diversion.

(2) The relevant road administrative authority decides on the closure and diversion based on the application of the person in whose interest the closure is to take place, after prior consent of the Ministry of the Interior, if it concerns motorways or expressways, in other cases after prior consent of the relevant body of the Police of the Czech Republic. The relevant road administrative authority shall discuss the application

- a) with the owner of the road to be closed and with the owner of the road along which the diversion is to be led,

- b) with the municipality in whose built-up area the closure is to be permitted or the diversion ordered,

- c) with the railway operator,^13) if it concerns a road on which a railway is located.

(3) If the closure concerns the territorial district of several road administrative authorities, the decision-making shall fall to the road administrative authority in whose territorial district the longest part of the closure is located; other road administrative authorities are in these cases affected administrative authorities.

(4) When making a decision, the relevant road administrative authority shall ensure that the closure is always limited to the shortest possible time, the diversion is properly technically secured and satisfactory from a traffic perspective, and that access to adjacent properties is enabled. The road administrative authority may specify conditions in the decision, upon non-fulfillment of which it may limit or revoke the granted permit. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect.

(5) The relevant road administrative authority shall promptly notify the decision to

- a) the relevant district fire protection authorities,

- b) the relevant district health service authorities,

- c) carriers in scheduled passenger transport, if it concerns a road on which such transport is operated and if these carriers are known to the road administrative authority,

- d) all road administrative authorities that permit the transport of particularly heavy or oversized objects, if it concerns the closure of a road used for this type of transport.

(6) The owner of a motorway, road, local road or publicly accessible účelová komunikace (access road) along which a diversion is to be led is obliged to tolerate, free of charge, traffic transferred to it from the closed road. Any necessary adjustments to the diversion roads due to the diversion and compensation for any subsequent damages shall be determined in the conditions of the decision on the closure and diversion and shall be carried out at the expense of the applicant for the closure and diversion.

(7) The closure and diversion must be marked in the prescribed manner. The marking is ensured by the applicant for the closure and diversion at their own expense and they are responsible for its condition for the duration of the closure and diversion.

(8) In case of danger in delay (natural disasters, traffic accidents, collapse or damage to objects), the owner of the relevant part of the road must immediately close and mark it at least provisionally. In case of a breakdown of utility networks or other lines, the owner of such utility network or line is obliged to immediately close and at least provisionally mark the relevant part of the road and immediately notify the owner of the road. This does not affect the provision of § 36 para. 6.

(9) The implementing regulation shall specify the requirements for an application for permission for a closure and for ordering a diversion and the requirements for decisions in these matters.

p25.heading: Special use
p25.text:
(1) The use of motorways, roads and local roads in a manner other than the usual one or for purposes other than those for which they are intended (hereinafter referred to as “special use”) requires a permit from the competent road administrative authority issued with the prior consent of the owner of the affected road and, if the special use may affect the safety or fluidity of road traffic, also with the prior consent of the Ministry of the Interior, in the case of motorways and expressways, in other cases with the consent of the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic.

(2) The road administrative authority may stipulate conditions for special use in the permit and, if these are not met or in the public interest, may restrict or revoke the granted permit. A permit for special use does not relieve the user of the obligation to compensate for damage or pollution of the motorway, road or local road. The permit does not replace a lease agreement for a part of the road land.

(3) During a state of national defence readiness, no permit for special use is required for vehicles of the Army of the Czech Republic.

(4) Special use of a motorway, road and local road includes:

- a) transport of exceptionally heavy or oversized objects and the use of vehicles whose dimensions or weight exceed the limits stipulated by special regulations,^10)

- b) use of a motorway, expressway or express local road by road motor vehicles whose maximum permitted speed is lower than stipulated by a special regulation,^2)

- c) lease of a part of the road land for

- 1. placing advertising hoardings, other promotional or advertising devices and other devices or objects, especially sales or entertainment ones,

- 2. placing, storing and loading items or materials not used for the maintenance or repair of these roads, if they are not removed immediately (construction site facilities, storage of building materials or fuel, etc.),

- 3. carrying out construction work,

- 4. establishing reserved parking,

- 5. establishing and operating stalls, mobile or portable sales and other similar facilities,

- 6. audiovisual production,

- d) placing utility networks and other above-ground or underground lines of all kinds in the road land, on it or on bridge structures,

- e) organizing sports, cultural, religious, entertainment and similar events and gatherings, if they could endanger the safety or fluidity of road traffic,

- f) exceptional use of a road or local road by tracked vehicles of the Army of the Czech Republic or historic vehicles whose wheels are not fitted with pneumatic tyres or rubber bands.

(5) Items placed without a permit according to paragraph 1 or in contravention thereof must be removed by their owner immediately after delivery of a request from the owner of the motorway, road or local road. If they fail to do so, the owner of the road shall remove and dispose of the item at the expense of the owner of the item. If the owner of the motorway, road or local road cannot ascertain the owner of the item, they shall proceed in accordance with the provisions of § 19 para. 4.

(6) The placing of items on a motorway, road and local road, which are listed in § 14 para. 1 letter b) and para. 2 letters b) to d), is not special use of the motorway, road and local road, but requires the prior consent of the owner of the affected road.

(7) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the particulars of the application for a permit for special use of a motorway, road and local road and the particulars of the decision in this matter.

Passability of motorways, passability and accessibility of roads and local roads and their securing

p26.text:
(1) Motorways, roads and local roads are passable if they allow for the safe movement of road and other vehicles adapted to the structural condition and traffic-technical condition of these roads and to weather situations and their consequences.

(2) Within the built-up area of a municipality, local roads and through sections of roads are accessible if they allow for the safe movement of pedestrians, which is movement adapted to the structural condition and traffic-technical condition of these roads and to weather situations and their consequences.

(3) The structural condition of a motorway, road or local road means their quality, degree of surface wear, longitudinal or transverse waves, potholes that cannot be removed by routine maintenance, bearing capacity of the carriageway, verges, bridges and bridge structures, and the equipment of the road with components and accessories.

(4) The traffic-technical condition of a motorway, road or local road means their technical characteristics (transverse arrangement, transverse and longitudinal gradient, width and type of carriageway, horizontal and vertical curves) and the integration of the road into the terrain (visibility, altitude).

(5) Weather situations and their consequences that can significantly worsen or interrupt passability include blizzards and intense prolonged snowfall, the formation of continuous ice, fog, thaws, freezing rain, gales, floods and flash floods, and other similar weather situations and their consequences.

(6) A defect in passability for the purposes of this Act means such a change in the passability of a motorway, road or local road that the driver of a vehicle cannot foresee when moving the vehicle in a manner adapted to the structural condition and traffic-technical condition of these roads and to weather situations and their consequences.

(7) A defect in accessibility for the purposes of this Act means such a change in the accessibility of a road that a pedestrian cannot foresee when moving in a manner adapted to the structural condition and traffic-technical condition and to weather situations and their consequences.

p27.text:
(1) Users of a motorway, road, or local road are not entitled to compensation for damage incurred due to the structural condition or traffic-technical condition of these land communications.

(2) The owner of a motorway, road, or local road is liable for damages incurred by users of these land communications, the cause of which was a defect in road usability, unless they prove that it was not within their power to remove this defect, or, in the case of a defect caused by weather conditions and their consequences, to mitigate such a defect, or to warn of it in the prescribed manner.

(3) The owner of a local road is liable, with the exception of cases specified in paragraph 4, for damages the cause of which was a defect in the walkability of the local road or the through section of a road, unless they prove that it was not within their power to remove this defect, or, in the case of a defect caused by weather conditions and their consequences, to mitigate such a defect, or to warn of it in the prescribed manner.

(4) The owner of real estate that borders a road or local road in the built-up area of a municipality is liable for damages the cause of which was a defect in the walkability of the adjacent pavement, which arose from pollution, black ice, or snow, unless they prove that it was not within their power to remove this defect; or, in the case of a defect caused by weather conditions and their consequences, to mitigate such a defect.

(5) The owner of a motorway, road, or local road is liable, according to general legal regulations, to the owners of adjacent real estate for damages incurred by them as a result of the structural condition or traffic-technical condition of these communications; however, they are not liable for damages incurred by the owners of adjacent real estate as a result of traffic on these land communications.

(6) Sections of roads and local roads where, due to their minor traffic significance, road usability and walkability are not ensured by removing snow and black ice, the owner is obliged to mark according to a special regulation^2) or an implementing regulation. The demarcation of such road sections shall be determined by the relevant district authority by its decree, and the demarcation of local road sections shall be determined by the relevant municipality by a generally binding ordinance.

(7) The implementing regulation shall further define the scope, method, and time limits for removing defects in the road usability of motorways, roads, and local roads. The municipality shall determine by a generally binding ordinance the scope, method, and time limits for removing defects in the walkability of local roads and through sections of roads.

p28.text:
(1) In the event of pollution of a motorway, road, or local road that causes or may cause defects in road usability or walkability, the person who caused the pollution must remove it without delay and restore the land communication to its original state; if this is not done, they are obliged to reimburse the owner of the land communication for the costs associated with removing the pollution and restoring the land communication to its original state. This is without prejudice to special regulations governing the handling of dangerous goods.^14)

(2) In the event of damage to a motorway, road, or local road that causes or may cause a defect in road usability or walkability or endanger road traffic safety according to a special regulation,^2) the person who caused the damage must immediately notify the owner of the land communication and reimburse them for the costs associated with removing the damage and restoring the land communication to its original state; they may also agree with the owner of the land communication to remove the damage themselves.

(3) If a defect in the road usability or walkability of a land communication cannot be removed immediately, the person who is obliged to remove the pollution or, as the case may be, to reimburse the costs of removing the damage, is obliged to at least provisionally mark the place immediately and notify the owner of the land communication of the defect.

p29.heading: Fixed Obstacles
p29.text:
(1) Only traffic signs and devices^2), with the exception of railings, mirrors, and call boxes, may be placed on the carriageways, traffic islands, and verges of a motorway, road, and local road; other objects constitute a fixed obstacle.

(2) A fixed obstacle may be placed on a land communication only on the basis of a permit issued by the road administrative authority after consultation with the owner of the affected land communication and with the consent of the Ministry of the Interior, in the case of a motorway and expressway, or in other cases with the consent of the relevant authority of the Police of the Czech Republic. A permit may be issued only on the condition that the safety and fluidity of road traffic will not be endangered and that the applicant for the permit will ensure all necessary measures at their own expense.

(3) Owners of fixed obstacles for which no permit has been issued are obliged to remove them at their own expense within the period specified by the road administrative authority. After the fruitless expiry of the specified period, the owner of the motorway, road, or local road is entitled to remove the fixed obstacle at the expense of its owner.

(4) If a fixed obstacle is a pole of an overhead telecommunication or energy line for which no permit from the road administrative authority has been issued, a permit for its placement may be issued according to paragraph 2, or the owner of the affected land communication shall agree on a period for its removal with the owner of the affected line; if no agreement on the removal period is reached, the court shall decide.

(5) If a fixed obstacle is a tree, the provisions of § 15 shall apply.

p30.text:
(1) Road protection zones serve to protect motorways, roads, and local roads of class I or II, and the traffic thereon, outside continuously built-up areas of municipalities. A road protection zone for a newly constructed or reconstructed motorway, road, and local road of class I or II arises on the basis of a decision on the placement of the structure.^5)

(2) For the purposes of this Act, a road protection zone means an area bounded by vertical planes extending up to a height of 50 m and at a distance of

- a) 100 m from the axis of the adjacent carriageway of a motorway, expressway, or express local road, or from the axis of a branch of their intersections; if the zone thus determined would not include the entire area of a rest stop, the boundary of the zone shall be the boundary of the road land,

- b) 50 m from the axis of the roadway or the adjacent carriageway of other class I roads and other class I local roads,

- c) 15 m from the axis of the roadway or from the axis of the adjacent carriageway of a class II or III road and a class II local road.

p31.text: Within the road protection zone, the establishment and operation of advertising billboards, promotional and other devices, light sources, colored surfaces, and other similar devices may be permitted only if they cannot be confused with traffic signs or traffic devices, or if they cannot dazzle users of the affected road. The permit is issued by the competent road administrative authority after prior consent from the Ministry of the Interior, in the case of motorways and expressways, and in other cases after prior consent from the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic.

p32.text:
(1) Within road protection zones, only on the basis of a permit issued by the road administrative authority and under the conditions specified in the permit, may

- a) constructions be carried out that, according to special regulations,^5) require a permit or notification to the building authority,

- b) terrain adjustments be carried out that would lower or raise the terrain level in relation to the roadway's grade line.

The provisions of this paragraph do not affect regulations on spatial planning and building regulations.^5)

(2) A permit according to the preceding paragraph is not required for constructions of bus stops for scheduled passenger transport, tram and trolleybus track facilities, telecommunication and energy lines, and for constructions related to the adjustment of drainage conditions.

p33.text: Within the road protection zone on the inner side of a road curve and local road of class I or II with a radius of 500 m or less, and in the sight triangles of level intersections of these roads, no objects may be established or operated, no trees or tall shrubs planted, and no crops cultivated that, by their growth and with regard to the terrain level, would obstruct the visibility necessary for road safety; this does not apply to forest stands with a shrub park ensuring the stability of the forest edge. The sides of the sight triangles are set at 100 m for a road marked with a traffic sign according to a special regulation^2) as a main road, and 55 m for a road marked with a traffic sign according to a special regulation^2) as a secondary road.

p34.text:
(1) The owner of a motorway, road, and local road of class I or II is entitled, within the road protection zone, for the necessary time and to the necessary extent, to enter foreign land, or structures standing thereon, for the purpose of repairs, maintenance, placement of snow fences, removal of consequences of accidents and other obstacles limiting road traffic. In doing so, they are obliged to ensure that the rights of owners are disturbed as little as possible and that no preventable damage arises from the entry and activities. If damage to the land or structure arises thereby, the person who caused the damage is obliged to compensate it; they cannot be relieved of this liability.

(2) If it concerns a road or its part for which no road protection zone is established, this provision applies to the entry of the road owner onto adjacent land.

(3) If the desired purpose cannot be achieved otherwise, the owner of a motorway, road, or local road is entitled, in a state of emergency or in an urgent public interest in maintaining the passability and accessibility of this road, for the necessary time, to the necessary extent, and for compensation, to use the real estate of the owner within the road protection zone, and if no road protection zone is established, the real estate of the adjacent owner.

p35.heading: Protection of motorways, roads, and local roads
p35.text:
(1) The road administrative authority identifies sources of threat to motorways, roads, and local roads and sources of disturbance to road traffic thereon. Upon identifying a source of threat, the road administrative authority shall order its operator or owner to remove the source of threat. If the operator or owner of the source of threat fails to comply, the road administrative authority shall decide on the removal of the source of threat at their expense.

(2) The owner of real estate adjacent to a through section of a road or a local road in a built-up area of a municipality is obliged, in necessarily urgent cases, for a one-time fee, to tolerate the establishment of an easement on their real estate consisting of the placement of public lighting, traffic signs and devices, and signs with local names. The decision on the establishment of the easement and on the amount of the fee is issued by the competent road administrative authority for traffic signs and devices based on a decision on their use issued by the authority competent according to a special regulation.^2)

p36.heading: Intersection of motorways, roads, and local roads with utilities and surroundings
p36.text:
(1) If public interest so requires, motorways, roads and local roads may intersect utility networks and other lines, waters, natural groundwater reserves, areas protected under special regulations,^15) water management and other works, or otherwise affect them, and may be intersected or otherwise affected by them, in a manner proportionate to environmental protection and local conditions, so as to affect the interests of the participating owners as little as possible.

(2) Energy, telecommunication, water supply, sewerage and other lines, heat and heating gas distribution facilities (hereinafter referred to as „lines“), with the exception of tram and trolleybus lines, if not established for the needs of a motorway, road or local road, may not be placed longitudinally in their body and on road auxiliary land, unless otherwise provided in further provisions.

(3) If it is not possible to place lines outside the road land without disproportionate costs, the longitudinal placement of such lines into the road auxiliary land, into the central dividing strip or onto bridges and bridge structures of the affected road may be permitted as a special use of the motorway, road or local road. Unless a special regulation^16) provides otherwise, the owner of the affected road shall, on the basis of the issued permit for special use, conclude an agreement with the owner of the line on the establishment of an easement to the road for a one-off payment. If an agreement is not reached, the court shall decide on the establishment of the easement and the amount of the payment.

(4) In built-up areas of a municipality, lines may be placed longitudinally, in addition to the cases specified in paragraph 3 and under the conditions specified in this paragraph, also in pavements and in adjacent green belts of the through section of a road or in a local road with the highest possible regard for vegetation. In cases where the possibility of another technical solution is excluded, lines may also be placed in the roadways of these roads.

(5) When carrying out construction works on a motorway, road or local road, which could damage lines, the owner of the line is obliged, at the request of the owner of the affected road, to provide the necessary documents and expert supervision free of charge.

(6) In the event of a line failure, no prior permit for special use of the motorway, road or local road is required for the necessary works to immediately remove such failure. The owner of the line is obliged to immediately notify the owner of the affected road of the works performed, at least provisionally mark and secure their location, and ensure the subsequent restoration of the affected section of the road to its original state according to the conditions set out in the additional permit for special use of the road.

(7) When constructing a new motorway, road or local road or reconstructing them, the client of this construction is obliged to pay only for the necessary induced modifications of the directly affected section of the line, at the level of the existing technical solution. Modifications related to the modernization or increase of the performance of the lines must be paid for by their owner.

(8) Unless this Act provides otherwise, special regulations^15) apply to the interface of motorways, roads and local roads with utility networks and other lines, with waters, natural groundwater reserves, with areas protected under special regulations, with mining areas and with water management and other works. Details on the method of placing, repairing and maintaining utility networks and other lines on road land are stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p37.heading: Interface with Railways
p37.text:
(1) The crossing of a road and a local road with a railway shall be established outside the level of the tracks. The establishment of a level crossing of a road or a local road with a railway (level crossing) may be permitted by the competent road administrative authority with the consent of the railway administrative authority and the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic only in cases of minor traffic significance specified by the implementing regulation. This provision does not apply to tram and trolleybus lines and to sidings.

(2) Safety devices at a level crossing with a railway shall be placed and maintained by the owner of the railway. The owner of the railway is obliged to maintain the road or local road at a level crossing without barriers in proper condition up to a distance of 2.5 m from the axis of the outermost track, at a level crossing with barriers in the section between the barriers, across the entire width of the road body. If the width of the road or local road at the level crossing does not correspond to the width of its adjacent sections, the owner of the railway is obliged to adequately widen the level crossing during its reconstruction; for roads and local roads narrower than 5 m, a clear width of at least 5 m must be maintained at the level crossing.

(3) For roads and local roads of Class II, the owner of the railway is obliged to ensure

- a) the adjustment of the level crossing enabling smooth entry of road vehicles,

- b) measures at the level crossing in continuously built-up areas of municipalities, so that pedestrians are not forced to use the roadway of the road or local road when crossing the railway,

- c) that the placed railway facilities do not obstruct the necessary visibility of users of the road or local road and, with the exception of overhead lines, do not extend into the space above their roadway up to a height of 5 m; facilities that do not comply with this must be relocated or removed during railway reconstruction.

(4) If it is technically necessary or if public interest so requires, in addition to tram and trolleybus lines, other railway lines may also be led along a road or a local road, in a manner proportionate to local conditions, so as to affect the interests of the participating owners and operators as little as possible and so that their mutual operation is not endangered. Any conflicts of interest shall be decided by the competent road administrative authority with the consent of the railway administrative authority.

(5) Unless this Act provides otherwise, special regulations^13) apply to the interface of motorways, roads and local roads with railways, to their crossings, the securing of these crossings, and to the use of roads and local roads for leading railway lines.

p38.heading: Use of Motorways, Roads and Local Roads during Large Constructions
p38.text:
(1) If, as a result of other investment construction, a part of a motorway, road or local road is to be abolished or public traffic on it is to be excluded for a long time, the client of such construction is obliged to build a replacement road at their own expense and transfer it free of charge to the owner of the original road that is to be abolished.

(2) If, during a major construction project or extensive mining work or earthworks requiring a building permit, or approval according to special regulations^5), a road or local road is to be used to an extent or in a manner that does not correspond to the structural condition or traffic-technical condition of these roads, the necessary modifications to the affected road, or the construction of a detour corresponding to the anticipated traffic, must be ensured by the client of the work and at their expense, in agreement with the owner of the road or local road.

(3) If an agreement is not reached, the relevant building authority shall decide on the scope of the necessary modifications to the affected road, or on the determination of construction site traffic routes or on the construction of a detour, based on the opinion of the relevant road administrative authority. The relevant road administrative authority is obliged to issue an opinion within 30 days of receiving the request for its preparation.

p39.heading: Extraordinary changes in traffic significance
p39.text: If there is a substantial increase in the load on a part of a road or local road, the structural condition or traffic-technical condition of which clearly does not correspond to this increase, the person who caused the increase is obliged to cover the costs associated with the necessary modification of the affected part of the road or local road. If an agreement on the amount of compensation is not reached with the owner of the affected part of the road or local road, the court shall decide.

p40.heading: Performance of state administration
p40.text:
(1) State administration in matters of motorways, roads, local roads and public utility roads is performed by road administrative authorities, which are the Ministry of Transport and Communications and district authorities. The scope of authority of a road administrative authority is also performed by municipalities to the extent stipulated by this Act.

(2) The Ministry of Transport and Communications

- a) decides on the classification of a road into the category of motorway or road and on changes to these categories,

- b) decides on the abolition of a motorway or road in agreement with the Ministry of Defence,

- c) performs the authority of a road administrative authority and a special building authority^4) according to this Act in matters of motorways and expressways,

- d) permits special use of roads in the form of transport of exceptionally heavy or oversized objects and the use of vehicles whose dimensions or weight exceed the limits stipulated by special regulations,^10) if the transport route extends beyond the territorial district of one district,

- e) decides on appeals against decisions of district authorities.

(3) District authorities

- a) perform the authority of a road administrative authority and a special building authority in matters of roads, with the exception of matters in which the Ministry of Transport and Communications decides,

- b) deal with infringements on motorways and roads according to this Act and according to a special regulation,^17)

- c) perform the authority of a special building authority in matters of local roads in municipalities that are not authorized to perform the authority of a general building authority according to special regulations,^5)

- d) decide on appeals against decisions of municipalities.

(4) Municipalities

- a) decide on the classification of a road into the category of local roads and on the declassification of a local road from this category,

- b) deal with infringements in matters of local roads and publicly accessible utility roads according to this Act and according to a special regulation,^17)

- c) perform the authority of a road administrative authority in matters of local roads and publicly accessible utility roads,

- d) perform the authority of a special building authority in matters of local roads, if they are authorized to perform the function of a general building authority according to a special regulation.^5)

p41.heading: State supervision
p41.text:
(1) State supervision over motorways, roads, local roads and publicly accessible utility roads is performed by road administrative authorities and municipalities within the scope of their authority. The Ministry of Transport and Communications performs supreme state supervision over the performance of state supervision carried out by district authorities and municipalities.

(2) A person authorized to perform state supervision monitors whether the owners (administrators) and users of roads fulfill the obligations stipulated by this Act. If the authorized person identifies a violation of the obligations stipulated by this Act during the performance of state supervision, they shall, as necessary and according to the nature of the identified deficiencies, issue a written order specifying the method and deadline for remedying these deficiencies and their causes.

(3) If the person authorized to perform state supervision identifies serious defects in the structural condition or in the traffic-technical condition of a road during the performance of supervision, which immediately endanger users or the road, they are authorized to issue a written order to the owner of this road for the immediate cessation or restriction of its use and shall notify the relevant road administrative authority, which is obliged to initiate the relevant administrative proceedings within two days.

(4) A person authorized to perform state supervision is obliged to present an identification card, which must contain their name, surname, and the substantive and territorial scope of their authorization. The model of this identification card shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(5) The authorizations and obligations according to paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 apply to persons authorized to perform supreme state supervision during the performance of supervision.

(6) Other rights and obligations of persons authorized to perform state supervision are stipulated by a special regulation.^18)

Penalties

p42.text:
(1) The road administration authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 200,000 on a legal entity that

- a) after written warning by a person authorized to perform state supervision, failed to fulfill the obligation to place or maintain a traffic sign or device,

- b) caused or failed to remove a defect in passability or accessibility, or in the case of a defect caused by natural situations and their consequences, failed to mitigate such a defect or failed to warn of the defect in the prescribed manner,

- c) unlawfully established, altered, or abolished an exit or entrance from a motorway, road, or local road to an adjacent property,

- d) during general use of a motorway, road, or local road, violated the prohibitions stipulated in § 19 para. 2 and 3,

- e) failed to remove a source of threat to a motorway, road, or local road or a source of disturbance to road traffic thereon,

- f) unlawfully placed a fixed obstacle on a road or failed to remove it as per a decision,

- g) without the consent of the road administration authority, carried out activities in the road protection zone that require such consent, or otherwise violated the regime stipulated for road protection zones,

- h) failed to close or mark a part of a road if there was a danger of delay due to an accident of utility networks or other lines, natural disasters, traffic accidents, and collapse or damage to objects,

- i) disobeyed an order of a person authorized to perform state supervision during the performance of such supervision.

(2) The road administration authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 500,000 on a legal entity that

- a) failed to perform duties related to the exercise of ownership rights until the transfer of the road to another owner upon a change in its category,

- b) used a motorway, road, or local road for special use without a permit or failed to comply with the conditions stipulated in the permit,

- c) unlawfully closed a motorway, road, local road, or publicly accessible service road, or unlawfully established a detour, or failed to mark a ordered closure or detour according to the issued decision, or failed to comply with the conditions stipulated by that decision.

p43.text:
(1) Proceedings for imposing a fine may be initiated only within one year from the day on which the road administration authority or municipality learned of the breach of obligations specified in § 42, but no later than three years from the day on which the breach occurred, and in the case of a continuing unauthorized breach, no later than three years from the time when the unauthorized activity was still ongoing. The road administration authority or municipality shall also collect the fine.

(2) When determining the amount of the fine, the road administration authority or municipality shall take into account the seriousness, significance, and duration of the unlawful activity, as well as the extent of the damage caused.

(3) Fines imposed and collected under this Act are revenue of the state budget. If a municipality imposes a fine, the fine is revenue of that municipality.

p44.heading: Joint Provisions
p44.text:
(1) Unless this Act provides otherwise, proceedings in matters regulated by this Act shall be conducted in accordance with general regulations on administrative proceedings.^19)

(2) When transferring a motorway or road from state ownership to municipal ownership in connection with a change in the category of the road pursuant to § 3 para. 3, and when transferring a motorway or road from state ownership to other persons pursuant to § 38 para. 1, regulations requiring special consent or exceptions for transfers of state property to other persons shall not apply.^20)

p45.heading: Transitional Provisions
p45.text:
(1) The provision of § 10 para. 3 does not apply to the connection of service roads and adjacent properties to motorways and expressways, if the decision of the relevant road administration authority on permitting these connections became final before the effective date of this Act.

(2) If valid spatial planning documentation was approved before 1 July 1992 and therefore the construction of motorways, roads, and express local roads was not defined as public utility structures in its binding part, these structures are public utility structures for the realization of which land, structures, and rights thereto may be expropriated according to special regulations.^5)

(3) For utilities that were already laid in or on road land on the effective date of this Act, it is not necessary to request a permit for special use of the road pursuant to § 25 para. 4 letter d); this does not affect the provision of § 29 para. 4.

p46.heading: Empowering Provisions
p46.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport and Communications shall issue legal regulations for the implementation of § 6, 8, 9, 10, 16, 20, 22, 24, 25, 27, 36, 37, and 41.

(2) The Ministry of Finance shall issue a legal regulation for the implementation of § 21.

p47.heading: Repealing Provisions
p47.text:
The following are repealed:

- 1. Act No. 135/1961 Coll., on Roads (Road Act), as amended by Act No. 27/1984 Coll., Act No. 213/1993 Coll., and Act No. 134/1994 Coll., with the exception of the provisions of § 6 para. 3, § 6a, 6b, and § 24 para. 1 letter l), which are repealed on 31 December 1997.

- 2. Decree of the Federal Ministry of Transport No. 35/1984 Coll., implementing the Act on Roads (Road Act).

- 3. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. SM-1976/77-23, issuing Guidelines on Preventing Damage and Settling Damage Caused by Combat Equipment of the Armed Forces on Roads (registered in Part 24/1977 Coll.), including the amendment issued on 10 April 1986 under Ref. No. 80/86.

- 4. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 14 078/82-025 on Motorway Closures (registered in Part 35/1982 Coll.).

- 5. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 23 462/83-25 on the Use of Roads for the Movement of Combat Equipment under a Special Regime (registered in Part 32/1983 Coll.).

- 6. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 22 130/1984-025 on the Control Badge of State Expert Supervision over Roads (registered in Part 16/1985 Coll.).

- 7. Edict of the Federal Ministry of Transport Ref. No. 8 013/1986-025 on the Maintenance of Motorways, Roads, and Local Roads (registered in Part 5/1986 Coll.).

p48.heading: Effectiveness
p48.text:
This Act shall enter into force on 1 April 1997, with the exception of the provisions of § 20, 21 and 22, which shall enter into force on 1 January 1998.

**Zeman m.p.**

**Havel m.p.**

**Klaus m.p.**

^3) § 8 (2) and (4), § 46 et seq. of Act No. 114/1992 Sb., on the protection of nature and landscape, as amended.

^4) § 120 of Act No. 50/1976 Sb., on territorial planning and the building code (Building Act), as amended.

^5) Act No. 50/1976 Sb., as amended.

^6) Act No. 114/1992 Sb., as amended. Act of the Czech National Council No. 244/1992 Sb., on environmental impact assessment.

^7) Decree of the Federal Ministry of Finance No. 122/1984 Sb., on compensation for the expropriation of buildings, land, vegetation and rights thereto.

^8) § 58 (2), § 139 (1) (b) of Act No. 50/1976 Sb.

^9) Act of the Czech National Council No. 565/1990 Sb., on local fees, as amended.

^10) Decree of the Ministry of Transport No. 102/1995 Sb., on the approval of technical roadworthiness and technical conditions for the operation of road vehicles on roadways.

^11) Decree of the Ministry of the Interior No. 145/1956 Ú. l., on road traffic, as amended.

^12) Act No. 526/1990 Sb., on prices, as amended by Act No. 135/1994 Sb.

^13) Act No. 266/1994 Sb.

^14) Act No. 111/1994 Sb., on road transport, as amended by Act No. 38/1995 Sb. Decree of the Minister of Foreign Affairs No. 64/1987 Sb., on the European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (ADR).

^16) Act No. 110/1964 Sb., as amended. Act No. 222/1994 Sb.

^17) § 23 of Act of the Czech National Council No. 200/1990 Sb., on misdemeanours, as amended.

^18) Act of the Czech National Council No. 552/1991 Sb., on state control, as amended by Act No. 166/1993 Sb.

^19) Act No. 71/1967 Sb., on administrative procedure (Administrative Procedure Code).

^20) § 45 (1) of Act No. 92/1991 Sb., on the conditions for the transfer of state property to other persons, as amended. § 14 of Decree of the Federal Ministry of Finance No. 119/1988 Sb., on the management of national property.
